author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, the psalm of the canticle of the dedication of the house, of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, a Psalm of the joy of the Resurrection, and the change, the renewing of the body to an immortal state, and not only of the Lord, but also of the whole Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the former Psalm the tabernacle was finished, wherein we dwell in the time of war: but now the house is dedicated, which will abide in peace everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is then whole Christ who speaketh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will exalt Thee, O Lord, for Thou hast taken Me up” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will praise Thy high Majesty, O Lord, for Thou hast taken Me up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast not made Mine enemies to rejoice over Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And those, who have so often endeavoured to oppress Me with various persecutions throughout the world, Thou hast not made to rejoice over Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, My God, I have cried unto Thee, and Thou hast healed Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, My God, I have cried unto Thee, and I no longer bear about a body enfeebled and sick by mortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, Thou hast brought back My Soul from hell, and Thou hast saved Me from them that go down into the pit” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast saved Me from the condition of profound darkness, and the lowest slough of corruptible flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing to the Lord, O ye saints of His.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The prophet seeing these future things, rejoiceth, and saith, “Sing to the Lord, O ye saints of His.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And make confession of the remembrance of His holiness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And make confession to Him, that He hath not forgotten the sanctification, wherewith He hath sanctified you, although all this intermediate period belong to your desires.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For in His indignation is wrath” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath avenged against you the first sin, for which you have paid by death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And life in His will.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And life eternal, whereunto you could not return by any strength of your own, hath He given, because He so would.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the evening weeping will tarry.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evening began, when the light of wisdom withdrew from sinful man, when he was condemned to death: from this evening weeping will tarry, as long as God’s people are, amid labours and temptations, awaiting the day of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And exultation in the morning.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even to the morning, when there will be the exultation of the resurrection, which hath shone forth by anticipation in the morning resurrection of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I said in my abundance, I shall not be moved for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I, that people which was speaking from the first, said in mine abundance, suffering now no more any want, “I shall not be moved for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, in Thy will Thou hast afforded strength unto my beauty” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that this my abundance, O Lord, is not of myself, but that in Thy will Thou hast afforded strength unto my beauty, I have learnt from this, “Thou turnedst away Thy Face from me, and I became troubled;” for Thou hast sometimes turned away Thy Face from the sinner, and I became troubled, when the illumination of Thy knowledge withdrew from me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Unto Thee, O Lord, will I cry, and unto my God will I pray” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And bringing to mind that time of my trouble and misery, and as it were established therein, I hear the voice of Thy First-Begotten, my Head, about to die for me, and saying “Unto Thee, O Lord, will I cry, and unto My God will I pray.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What profit” is there in the shedding of My blood, whilst I go down to corruption?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall dust confess unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if I shall not rise immediately, and My body shall become corrupt, “shall dust confess unto Thee?” that is, the crowd of the ungodly, whom I shall justify by My resurrection?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Or declare Thy truth?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or for the salvation of the rest declare Thy truth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath heard, and had mercy on Me, the Lord hath become My helper.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor did “He suffer His holy One to see corruption” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast turned My mourning into joy to Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom I, the Church, having received, the First-Begotten from the dead, now in the dedication of Thine house, say, “Thou hast turned my mourning into joy to me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast torn off the veil of my sins, the sadness of my mortality; and hast girded me with the first robe, with immortal gladness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That my glory should sing unto Thee, and I should not be pricked” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That now, not my humiliation, but my glory should not lament, but should sing unto Thee, for that now out of humiliation Thou hast exalted me; and that I should not be pricked with the consciousness of sin, with the fear of death, with the fear of judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, my God, I will confess unto Thee for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is my glory, O Lord, my God, that I should confess unto Thee for ever, that I have nothing of myself, but that all my good is of Thee, who art “God, All in all.”
